justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch identified with explicit math (200+150+800=1150ms > 500ms), (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described (upstream times out, downstream continues, wasted work amplifies load), (3) Outbound > inbound relationship identified and called out as 'textbook timeout inversion' (1000ms each vs 500ms inbound), (4) Multiple remediation options provided (concurrent calls, reduced outbound timeouts, partial/cached enrichment, deadline propagation, circuit breakers), (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional depth — computed sequential p99 sum (1150ms) and called it '2.3x the budget', making the impossibility unambiguous
Identified the cascading failure mode: slow InventoryService → thread pool exhaustion → poisoning healthy calls
Correctly noted that retries would make things worse given zero remaining budget — a nuanced insight
Provided concrete timeout budget math for remediation: concurrent calls with ~400ms outbound timeout reserving 100ms for overhead
Escalation routing is well-targeted — architect for call pattern redesign, implementer for circuit breakers and observability
Identified that even parallelized calls (max 800ms) still exceed 500ms inbound timeout, showing the problem requires multiple fixes

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with explicit computation 200+150+800=1150ms > 500ms inbound. (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described — upstream disconnects at 500ms while enrichment service continues wasting compute and downstream capacity. (3) Outbound timeout (1000ms) > inbound timeout (500ms) relationship explicitly called out as 'CRITICALLY MISCONFIGURED'. (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, inbound timeout renegotiation, outbound timeout reduction, graceful degradation with partial results, context cancellation/deadline propagation. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional depth — computed not just the total p99 sum but also the remaining headroom after each sequential call, showing InventoryService starts with only 150ms left
Correctly noted that even parallelized (max 800ms), the calls still exceed 500ms inbound timeout, requiring additional mitigations
Identified circuit breaker absence as a compounding risk beyond the timeout issue itself
Raised the graceful degradation question — can orders proceed with partial enrichment — which is the pragmatic production solution
Escalations are well-routed: architect (MJ) for call pattern redesign, implementer (Shaq) for circuit breakers and observability
Observability gaps are specific and actionable — per-service latency histograms, timeout-specific counters, distributed tracing spans

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with exact arithmetic (200+150+800=1150ms > 500ms). (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence explicitly described — service continues processing up to 3000ms after caller gives up at 500ms, called 'pure waste' with recommendation for deadline propagation. (3) Outbound > inbound relationship identified and called 'CRITICALLY MISCONFIGURED' (1000ms each vs 500ms inbound). (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, tighter per-call timeouts, shared deadline budgeting, making InventoryService async/optional, graceful degradation with partial enrichment. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the timeout cascade analysis — not only identified the mismatch but computed that InventoryService alone (800ms) exceeds the budget even without the other calls
Correctly identified the orphaned request problem and recommended deadline propagation as the fix
Went beyond rubric requirements by identifying connection pool exhaustion, bulkhead needs, and health check coupling as additional failure modes
Smart observation that retries should NOT be added until the timeout architecture is fixed first
Appropriate escalation routing — architectural timeout fix to MJ, circuit breaker implementation to Shaq
Concrete timeout recommendations (150ms per-call parallel, or 400ms shared deadline with 100ms overhead reserve)
